entries.summary: Autoload cross-references fail at parse time
entries.detail: SaveManager.gd referenced GameState and Constants by name. GDScript parser rejects these at parse time even though autoloads are registered. Error: 'Identifier not declared in the current scope'.
entries.root_cause: GDScript validates identifiers during parsing, before autoloads are injected into the scene tree at runtime.
entries.resolution: Use get_node('/root/GameState') at runtime instead of bare autoload names.
entries.transfer_value: high — affects any script that references another autoload

entries.summary: Character flip not visible — 3 failed approaches
entries.detail: Attempt 1: _r() helper with flip multiplier — draw_rect() doesn't accept negative width, rects disappeared. Attempt 2: draw_set_transform() with scale Vector2(-1,1) — worked for drawing but Camera2D child also got affected. Attempt 3: scale.x on CharacterBody2D — flipped Camera2D, mirroring entire screen view. Final fix: child Node2D with scale.x = -1.
entries.root_cause: Transforms on parent nodes propagate to all children including Camera2D and CollisionShape2D. Only a dedicated visual child node should be transformed.
entries.resolution: Separate visual node (Node2D child) from physics body. Flip only the visual node's scale.x.
entries.transfer_value: high — fundamental pattern for any 2D character

entries.detail: Used sin(walk_cycle) to continuously interpolate leg/arm positions. User feedback: 'not behaving in a way that's anchored in physics', 'limbs aren't moving in any natural way', 'losing optimism in Godot'.
entries.root_cause: Continuous interpolation of individual rect positions creates smooth but disconnected motion. Pixel art characters need discrete keyframe poses, not procedural animation.
entries.resolution: Replaced with 4 discrete hand-drawn poses (idle, step_a, idle, step_b) swapped at fixed intervals.
entries.transfer_value: high — applies to all _draw() character animation

entries.summary: Player couldn't jump between floors — hit ceiling
entries.detail: Floor platforms were solid StaticBody2Ds. Player jumped but collided with the bottom of the floor above and bounced off.
entries.root_cause: Default collision is two-sided. Platformer floors need one_way_collision so player passes through from below.
entries.resolution: Set one_way_collision = true on all floor CollisionShape2Ds.
entries.transfer_value: high — required for any platformer with stacked floors

entries.summary: Jump height insufficient for floor spacing
entries.detail: JUMP_VELOCITY=-350, GRAVITY=900. Peak height = 350²/(2×900) = 68px. Floor spacing = 96px. Player couldn't reach the next floor.
entries.root_cause: Physics values were estimated without calculating actual peak height.
entries.resolution: Bumped JUMP_VELOCITY to -500. Peak = 500²/1800 = 139px, clears 96px comfortably.
entries.transfer_value: medium — always calculate peak = v²/(2g) when tuning jump

entries.summary: Player invisible after adding floor backgrounds
entries.detail: Floor ColorRect backgrounds were added via _ready() after the player scene was instanced. Default z_index rendering caused floors to draw over the player.
entries.root_cause: Godot 2D renders by scene tree order within the same z_index. Dynamically-added nodes render after scene-tree nodes.
entries.resolution: Set z_index = 10 on the player's Sprite node.
entries.transfer_value: medium — any dynamic scene content needs explicit z-ordering
